question.english: What are the types of food that people eat in your country?
question.chinese: 主題：你國家的人會吃哪些種類的食物？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the types of food that people eat in your country?
主題：你國家的人會吃哪些種類的食物？

components.english: In Hong Kong, people eat a very wide range of food, but I would say the heart of it is still Cantonese food and other kinds of Chinese cuisine.
components.chinese: 在香港，人們吃的食物種類非常多，但如果要我說核心是甚麼，我會說仍然是粵菜以及其他不同類型的中菜。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, people eat a very wide range of food, but I would say the
heart of it is still Cantonese food and other kinds of Chinese cuisine.
[在香港，人們吃的食物種類非常多，但如果要我說核心是甚麼，我會說仍然是粵菜以及其
他不同類型的中菜。]

components.english: Food in Hong Kong is incredibly varied, but Cantonese cooking still sits at the centre of everyday life. People commonly eat rice, noodles, congee, soup, roast meats, seafood, and dim sum, and those dishes are woven into daily routines almost without people even thinking about it. In a way, food in Hong Kong is like the city itself: fast-moving, layered, and full of character. From a simple bowl of wonton noodles to a big family meal in a Chinese restaurant, the range is broad, but the Cantonese thread still runs through it.
components.chinese: 香港的飲食非常多元，但粵式烹調仍然處於日常生活的中心位置。人們平常很常吃飯、麵、粥、湯、燒味、海鮮和點心，而這些食物幾乎已經自然地織進了日常生活之中，很多人甚至不會特別去想它。某程度上，香港的食物就像這座城市本身：節奏快、有層次，而且很有性格。由一碗簡單的雲吞麵，到一家人在酒樓裡吃的一頓中式晚飯，形式雖然很多，但粵菜這條主線始終都在。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Food in Hong Kong is incredibly varied, but Cantonese cooking
still sits at the centre of everyday life. People commonly eat rice, noodles, congee,
soup, roast meats, seafood, and dim sum, and those dishes are woven into daily
routines almost without people even thinking about it. In a way, food in Hong
Kong is like the city itself: fast-moving, layered, and full of character. From a
simple bowl of wonton noodles to a big family meal in a Chinese restaurant, the
range is broad, but the Cantonese thread still runs through it.
[香港的飲食非常多元，但粵式烹調仍然處於日常生活的中心位置。人們平常很常吃飯、
麵、粥、湯、燒味、海鮮和點心，而這些食物幾乎已經自然地織進了日常生活之中，很多人甚
至不會特別去想它。某程度上，香港的食物就像這座城市本身：節奏快、有層次，而且很有
性格。由一碗簡單的雲吞麵，到一家人在酒樓裡吃的一頓中式晚飯，形式雖然很多，但粵菜
這條主線始終都在。]

components.english: For example, many people may start the day with macaroni soup, toast, or milk tea in a cha chaan teng, have rice or noodles for lunch, and then eat stir-fried dishes, steamed fish, or roast duck for dinner. On weekends, dim sum is still a classic choice for family gatherings.
components.chinese: 例如，很多人早上可能會在茶餐廳吃通粉、多士或奶茶；中午吃飯或麵；到了晚上，則可能會吃小炒、蒸魚，或者燒鴨之類的菜式。到了週末，飲茶吃點心仍然是很多家庭聚會的經典選擇。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, many people may start the day with macaroni soup,
toast, or milk tea in a cha chaan teng, have rice or noodles for lunch, and then
eat stir-fried dishes, steamed fish, or roast duck for dinner. On weekends, dim
sum is still a classic choice for family gatherings.
[例如，很多人早上可能會在茶餐廳吃通粉、多士或奶茶；中午吃飯或麵；到了晚上，則可能
會吃小炒、蒸魚，或者燒鴨之類的菜式。到了週末，飲茶吃點心仍然是很多家庭聚會的經典
選擇。]

components.english: So, one major type of food people eat in Hong Kong is traditional Cantonese-style food, which is still very much the backbone of local eating habits.
components.chinese: 所以，香港人其中一大類常吃的食物，就是傳統粵式食物，而這仍然是本地飲食習慣的骨幹。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major type of food people eat in Hong Kong is
traditional Cantonese-style food, which is still very much the backbone of local
eating habits.
[所以，香港人其中一大類常吃的食物，就是傳統粵式食物，而這仍然是本地飲食習慣的骨

components.english: That said, Hong Kong people also eat a huge amount of international food, because the city has always been very open to outside influences.
components.chinese: 不過，香港人同時也吃很多國際化食物，因為這座城市一直都很容易吸收外來影響。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, Hong Kong people also eat a huge amount of international food, because
the city has always been very open to outside influences.
[不過，香港人同時也吃很多國際化食物，因為這座城市一直都很容易吸收外來影響。]

components.english: One of the most interesting things about Hong Kong is that its food scene is a real melting pot. Japanese, Korean, Thai, Vietnamese, Western, Indian, and all sorts of fusion food are everywhere. People here are used to eating across cultures, and that has become second nature. You can have sushi one day, hotpot the next, and pasta the day after that without batting an eyelid. In other words, local taste buds are not stuck in one lane; they travel widely.
components.chinese: 香港飲食最有趣的地方之一，就是它本身真的是一個大熔爐。日本菜、韓國菜、泰國菜、越南菜、西餐、印度菜，以及各種混合式料理，到處都看得到。香港人本來就很習慣跨文化地吃東西，而這件事早已變得很自然。你今天可以吃壽司，明天吃火鍋，後天再吃意粉，大家都覺得完全正常。換句話說，本地人的味蕾並不是只走一條路，而是走得很廣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: One of the most interesting things about Hong Kong is that
its food scene is a real melting pot. Japanese, Korean, Thai, Vietnamese,
Western, Indian, and all sorts of fusion food are everywhere. People here are
used to eating across cultures, and that has become second nature. You can have
sushi one day, hotpot the next, and pasta the day after that without batting an
eyelid. In other words, local taste buds are not stuck in one lane; they travel
widely.
[香港飲食最有趣的地方之一，就是它本身真的是一個大熔爐。日本菜、韓國菜、泰國菜、越
南菜、西餐、印度菜，以及各種混合式料理，到處都看得到。香港人本來就很習慣跨文化地
吃東西，而這件事早已變得很自然。你今天可以吃壽司，明天吃火鍋，後天再吃意粉，大家
都覺得完全正常。換句話說，本地人的味蕾並不是只走一條路，而是走得很廣。]

components.english: For instance, it is very common for people in Hong Kong to eat ramen, pizza, Thai curry, or Korean barbecue just as casually as they would eat fried rice or wonton noodles. Younger people in particular often mix local favourites with global trends, so their eating habits can be very flexible and adventurous.
components.chinese: 例如，在香港，人們很常會像吃炒飯或雲吞麵那樣自然地去吃拉麵、薄餅、泰式咖喱，或者韓式燒烤。尤其是年輕人，更常把本地喜好和全球潮流混在一起，所以他們的飲食習慣往往很靈活，也很願意嘗試新東西。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, it is very common for people in Hong Kong to eat
ramen, pizza, Thai curry, or Korean barbecue just as casually as they would eat
fried rice or wonton noodles. Younger people in particular often mix local
favourites with global trends, so their eating habits can be very flexible and
adventurous.
[例如，在香港，人們很常會像吃炒飯或雲吞麵那樣自然地去吃拉麵、薄餅、泰式咖喱，或者
韓式燒烤。尤其是年輕人，更常把本地喜好和全球潮流混在一起，所以他們的飲食習慣往往
很靈活，也很願意嘗試新東西。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong eat both traditional local food and a great deal of international food. That is what makes the city’s food culture so rich: it has deep roots, but it also has an open palate.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港人既吃很多傳統本地食物，也吃大量國際化食物。而這正正是香港飲食文化迷人的地方：它一方面根扎得很深，另一方面又有一個很開放的胃口。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong eat both traditional local
food and a great deal of international food. That is what makes the city’s food
culture so rich: it has deep roots, but it also has an open palate.
[總結來說，我會說香港人既吃很多傳統本地食物，也吃大量國際化食物。而這正正是香港
飲食文化迷人的地方：它一方面根扎得很深，另一方面又有一個很開放的胃口。]

question.english: What kinds of foreign food are popular in your country?
question.chinese: 你國家裡，哪些外國菜比較受歡迎？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kinds of foreign food are popular in your country?
你國家裡，哪些外國菜比較受歡迎？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say Japanese, Korean, and Thai food are especially popular, and they have really become part of everyday life.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說日本菜、韓國菜和泰國菜特別受歡迎，而且它們其實已經很大程度上融入了日常生活。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say Japanese, Korean, and Thai food are especially
popular, and they have really become part of everyday life.
[在香港，我會說日本菜、韓國菜和泰國菜特別受歡迎，而且它們其實已經很大程度上融入
了日常生活。]

components.english: The main reason is that people here are quite open to different flavours, and Hong Kong has always had a strong food culture. Japanese food appeals to people because it can be both light and refined, while Korean food often wins people over with its bold flavours and lively, social style of eating. Thai food, on the other hand, is loved for its punchy mix of sweet, sour, spicy, and savoury flavours. In a city where people are always on the move, food that is tasty, varied, and easy to find tends to go down very well.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，香港人對不同味道本來就相當開放，而香港本身也一直有很強的飲食文化。日本菜受歡迎，是因為它可以同時給人清淡又精緻的感覺；韓國菜則常常憑著濃郁味道和熱鬧的共食風格吸引人。至於泰國菜，人們喜歡它，往往是因為它把甜、酸、辣、鹹幾種味道融合得很有衝擊力。在一個大家總是步伐很快的城市裡，那些好吃、多樣，而且容易找到的食物，通常都很容易受歡迎。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that people here are quite open to different
flavours, and Hong Kong has always had a strong food culture. Japanese food
appeals to people because it can be both light and refined, while Korean food
often wins people over with its bold flavours and lively, social style of eating.
Thai food, on the other hand, is loved for its punchy mix of sweet, sour, spicy,
and savoury flavours. In a city where people are always on the move, food that is
tasty, varied, and easy to find tends to go down very well.
[主要原因是，香港人對不同味道本來就相當開放，而香港本身也一直有很強的飲食文化。
日本菜受歡迎，是因為它可以同時給人清淡又精緻的感覺；韓國菜則常常憑著濃郁味道和
熱鬧的共食風格吸引人。至於泰國菜，人們喜歡它，往往是因為它把甜、酸、辣、鹹幾種味道
融合得很有衝擊力。在一個大家總是步伐很快的城市裡，那些好吃、多樣，而且容易找到的
食物，通常都很容易受歡迎。]

components.english: For example, it is very common to see sushi shops, ramen restaurants, Korean barbecue places, and Thai noodle or curry restaurants across shopping malls and neighbourhood streets. Even on delivery apps, these cuisines are everywhere. If a food is available on every corner and people keep going back for more, that usually says it all.
components.chinese: 例如，在商場和社區街道上，看見壽司店、拉麵店、韓燒店，以及泰式粉麵或咖喱餐廳，其實是非常普遍的事。就連外賣平台上，這些菜式也幾乎隨處可見。如果一種食物在街頭巷尾都找得到，而且人們還是一吃再吃，那其實已經很能說明它有多受歡迎了。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, it is very common to see sushi shops, ramen
restaurants, Korean barbecue places, and Thai noodle or curry restaurants
across shopping malls and neighbourhood streets. Even on delivery apps, these
cuisines are everywhere. If a food is available on every corner and people keep
going back for more, that usually says it all.
[例如，在商場和社區街道上，看見壽司店、拉麵店、韓燒店，以及泰式粉麵或咖喱餐廳，其
實是非常普遍的事。就連外賣平台上，這些菜式也幾乎隨處可見。如果一種食物在街頭巷尾
都找得到，而且人們還是一吃再吃，那其實已經很能說明它有多受歡迎了。]

components.english: So, one clear pattern is that Asian foreign cuisines, especially Japanese, Korean, and Thai food, have become firm favourites in Hong Kong.
components.chinese: 所以，一個很明顯的現象就是，亞洲外國菜，特別是日本菜、韓國菜和泰國菜，已經成為香港人非常穩固的心頭好。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear pattern is that Asian foreign cuisines, especially
Japanese, Korean, and Thai food, have become firm favourites in Hong Kong.
[所以，一個很明顯的現象就是，亞洲外國菜，特別是日本菜、韓國菜和泰國菜，已經成為香
港人非常穩固的心頭好。]

components.english: On top of that, Western food is also very popular, especially Italian food, American-style fast food, and café-style meals.
components.chinese: 除此之外，西式食物也很受歡迎，特別是意大利菜、美式快餐，以及 café 風格的餐點。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, Western food is also very popular, especially Italian
food, American-style fast food, and café-style meals.
[除此之外，西式食物也很受歡迎，特別是意大利菜、美式快餐，以及 café 風格的餐點。]

components.english: I think that is partly because Western food fits different occasions very well. Pizza and pasta are popular for casual meals, burgers and fried chicken are easy comfort food, and brunch culture has become quite fashionable, especially among young people. In a way, Hong Kong’s food scene is a bit like an international menu with a local accent: people enjoy foreign dishes, but they also adapt them to their own tastes and routines. So foreign food here is not just imported; it is often absorbed, adjusted, and made part of the city’s rhythm.
components.chinese: 我認為這一方面是因為西式食物很適合不同場合。薄餅和意粉很適合輕鬆聚餐，漢堡包和炸雞則屬於方便又有安慰感的食物，而早午餐文化近年也變得相當流行，特別是在年輕人之間。某程度上，香港的飲食環境有點像一份帶著本地口音的國際菜單：人們喜歡外國菜，但同時也會按自己的口味和生活節奏去調整它們。所以，外國菜在香港不只是被引進而已，它很多時候也會被吸收、被改造，然後慢慢變成這座城市日常節奏的一部分。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think that is partly because Western food fits different
occasions very well. Pizza and pasta are popular for casual meals, burgers and
fried chicken are easy comfort food, and brunch culture has become quite
fashionable, especially among young people. In a way, Hong Kong’s food scene is
a bit like an international menu with a local accent: people enjoy foreign dishes,
but they also adapt them to their own tastes and routines. So foreign food here
is not just imported; it is often absorbed, adjusted, and made part of the city’s
rhythm.
[我認為這一方面是因為西式食物很適合不同場合。薄餅和意粉很適合輕鬆聚餐，漢堡包和
炸雞則屬於方便又有安慰感的食物，而早午餐文化近年也變得相當流行，特別是在年輕人
之間。某程度上，香港的飲食環境有點像一份帶著本地口音的國際菜單：人們喜歡外國菜，
但同時也會按自己的口味和生活節奏去調整它們。所以，外國菜在香港不只是被引進而已，
它很多時候也會被吸收、被改造，然後慢慢變成這座城市日常節奏的一部分。]

components.english: For instance, it is very normal for friends to meet for pasta or brunch at the weekend, or for office workers to grab a sandwich, salad, or burger during a busy lunch break. International bakery items, such as croissants or cheesecakes, are also hugely popular. They may not feel foreign anymore, but that is probably the strongest sign of all that they have really taken root.
components.chinese: 例如，朋友在週末約出去吃意粉或早午餐，或者上班族在忙碌的午飯時間買個三文治、沙律，或者漢堡包，其實都非常普遍。像牛角包、芝士蛋糕這類國際化烘焙食品也同樣很受歡迎。它們也許已經不再讓人覺得很「外國」，但這反而可能正是它們真正紮根下來的最強證明。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, it is very normal for friends to meet for pasta or
brunch at the weekend, or for office workers to grab a sandwich, salad, or
burger during a busy lunch break. International bakery items, such as croissants
or cheesecakes, are also hugely popular. They may not feel foreign anymore, but
that is probably the strongest sign of all that they have really taken root.
[例如，朋友在週末約出去吃意粉或早午餐，或者上班族在忙碌的午飯時間買個三文治、沙
律，或者漢堡包，其實都非常普遍。像牛角包、芝士蛋糕這類國際化烘焙食品也同樣很受歡
迎。它們也許已經不再讓人覺得很「外國」，但這反而可能正是它們真正紮根下來的最強證

components.english: Overall, I’d say a wide range of foreign food is popular in Hong Kong, from Japanese and Korean dishes to Italian meals and American fast food. The city’s palate is quite international, so people are usually happy to try different cuisines as long as the food is good, convenient, and worth coming back for.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，在香港，很多不同類型的外國菜都很受歡迎，從日本菜、韓國菜，到意大利餐和美式快餐都有。這座城市的口味本來就相當國際化，所以只要食物好吃、方便，而且值得再吃一次，人們通常都很願意接受不同地方的菜式。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a wide range of foreign food is popular in Hong
Kong, from Japanese and Korean dishes to Italian meals and American fast food.
The city’s palate is quite international, so people are usually happy to try
different cuisines as long as the food is good, convenient, and worth coming
back for.
[總結來說，我會說，在香港，很多不同類型的外國菜都很受歡迎，從日本菜、韓國菜，到意
大利餐和美式快餐都有。這座城市的口味本來就相當國際化，所以只要食物好吃、方便，而
且值得再吃一次，人們通常都很願意接受不同地方的菜式。]

question.english: In your country, is it important to have a meal together with your family?
question.chinese: 在你的國家，和家人一起吃飯重要嗎？

source_lines.text: In your country, is it important to have a meal together with your family?
在你的國家，和家人一起吃飯重要嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think it is important, especially in Hong Kong, because having a meal together is one of the simplest and most meaningful ways for a family to stay connected.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為這很重要，尤其在香港，因為一家人一起吃飯，是維繫家人關係最簡單、也最有意義的方法之一。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think it is important, especially in Hong Kong, because having
a meal together is one of the simplest and most meaningful ways for a family to
stay connected.
[是的，我的確認為這很重要，尤其在香港，因為一家人一起吃飯，是維繫家人關係最簡單、
也最有意義的方法之一。]

components.english: In everyday life, people are often pulled in different directions by work, school, and other responsibilities, so shared mealtimes become a chance to slow down and reconnect. When family members sit around the same table, they are not just eating; they are catching up, checking in, and keeping their relationships warm. In that sense, a family meal is a bit like an anchor in the day: it gives people a moment of stability in the middle of a busy routine.
components.chinese: 在日常生活中，人們往往會被工作、學業，以及其他責任拉向不同方向，所以一起吃飯就成了一個放慢腳步、重新連結彼此的機會。當家人坐在同一張桌子旁時，他們不只是吃飯；他們同時也在交流近況、彼此關心，並維持關係的溫度。從這個角度來說，一頓家庭飯有點像一天中的錨點：它在忙碌的生活節奏裡，給人一個穩定下來的時刻。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In everyday life, people are often pulled in different directions
by work, school, and other responsibilities, so shared mealtimes become a chance
to slow down and reconnect. When family members sit around the same table,
they are not just eating; they are catching up, checking in, and keeping their
relationships warm. In that sense, a family meal is a bit like an anchor in the
day: it gives people a moment of stability in the middle of a busy routine.
[在日常生活中，人們往往會被工作、學業，以及其他責任拉向不同方向，所以一起吃飯就
成了一個放慢腳步、重新連結彼此的機會。當家人坐在同一張桌子旁時，他們不只是吃飯；
他們同時也在交流近況、彼此關心，並維持關係的溫度。從這個角度來說，一頓家庭飯有點
像一天中的錨點：它在忙碌的生活節奏裡，給人一個穩定下來的時刻。]

components.english: For example, parents may ask their children how school went, children may talk about something that upset or excited them, and adults may share what happened at work. Those conversations may sound ordinary, but very often they are where family bonds are quietly strengthened.
components.chinese: 例如，父母可能會問孩子今天上學怎樣，孩子也可能會說起一些令自己不開心或很興奮的事情，而大人則可能分享工作上的近況。這些對話聽起來也許很平常，但很多時候，家人的感情正正就是在這些平凡交流裡慢慢加深的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, parents may ask their children how school went,
children may talk about something that upset or excited them, and adults may
share what happened at work. Those conversations may sound ordinary, but
very often they are where family bonds are quietly strengthened.
[例如，父母可能會問孩子今天上學怎樣，孩子也可能會說起一些令自己不開心或很興奮的
事情，而大人則可能分享工作上的近況。這些對話聽起來也許很平常，但很多時候，家人的
感情正正就是在這些平凡交流裡慢慢加深的。]

components.english: So yes, I would say eating together is important, because it gives families regular time to talk, listen, and feel close to one another.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說一家人一起吃飯是重要的，因為它讓家人有固定的時間去交流、聆聽，並感受到彼此的親近。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say eating together is important, because it gives
families regular time to talk, listen, and feel close to one another.
[所以，是的，我會說一家人一起吃飯是重要的，因為它讓家人有固定的時間去交流、聆聽，
並感受到彼此的親近。]

components.english: That said, I also think it has become harder to do this regularly nowadays, especially in a fast-paced place like Hong Kong.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，在今天要經常做到這一點，其實變得更困難了，尤其是在像香港這樣節奏很快的地方。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think it has become harder to do this regularly
nowadays, especially in a fast-paced place like Hong Kong.
[不過，我也認為，在今天要經常做到這一點，其實變得更困難了，尤其是在像香港這樣節
奏很快的地方。]

components.english: Many people work long hours, students often have packed schedules, and some family members may not even get home at the same time. So having a meal together every single day is not always realistic. However, I would argue that this makes shared meals more valuable, not less. When something is hard to make time for, it usually shows what matters. Even if families cannot manage it daily, making space for it when they can still helps keep the emotional thread from wearing thin.
components.chinese: 很多人工作時間很長，學生的時間表也常常排得很滿，而且有些家人甚至未必會在同一時間回到家。所以，要每天都一起吃飯並不總是那麼現實。不過，我反而會認為，正因如此，共同進餐才顯得更有價值，而不是更沒價值。當一件事情很難抽時間去做時，往往更能反映它的重要性。即使家庭未必能夠天天做到，只要能在可行的時候騰出空間去一起吃飯，仍然能幫助那條情感上的連線不要慢慢變薄。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Many people work long hours, students often have packed
schedules, and some family members may not even get home at the same time. So
having a meal together every single day is not always realistic. However, I would
argue that this makes shared meals more valuable, not less. When something is
hard to make time for, it usually shows what matters. Even if families cannot
manage it daily, making space for it when they can still helps keep the emotional
thread from wearing thin.
[很多人工作時間很長，學生的時間表也常常排得很滿，而且有些家人甚至未必會在同一時
間回到家。所以，要每天都一起吃飯並不總是那麼現實。不過，我反而會認為，正因如此，共
同進餐才顯得更有價值，而不是更沒價值。當一件事情很難抽時間去做時，往往更能反映
它的重要性。即使家庭未必能夠天天做到，只要能在可行的時候騰出空間去一起吃飯，仍然
能幫助那條情感上的連線不要慢慢變薄。]

components.english: For instance, some families may not be able to eat together on weekdays, but they still make an effort to have dinner together at weekends or gather for special occasions and traditional festivals. Those meals often mean more than the food on the table; they remind people that, however busy life gets, family is still home base.
components.chinese: 例如，有些家庭未必能在平日一起吃飯，但他們仍然會努力在週末一起吃晚飯，或者在特別場合和傳統節日聚在一起。那些飯局很多時候所代表的，已經不只是桌上的食物；它們提醒人們，不管生活有多忙，家庭始終是最根本的歸屬。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some families may not be able to eat together on
weekdays, but they still make an effort to have dinner together at weekends or
gather for special occasions and traditional festivals. Those meals often mean
more than the food on the table; they remind people that, however busy life
gets, family is still home base.
[例如，有些家庭未必能在平日一起吃飯，但他們仍然會努力在週末一起吃晚飯，或者在特
別場合和傳統節日聚在一起。那些飯局很多時候所代表的，已經不只是桌上的食物；它們提
醒人們，不管生活有多忙，家庭始終是最根本的歸屬。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it is still very important to have a meal together with your family in Hong Kong, even if it cannot happen all the time. A shared meal may look small from the outside, but quite often it is where a family becomes a family again.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說在香港，和家人一起吃飯仍然是很重要的，即使那未必能夠時時刻刻發生。一頓共同進餐從外表看來也許很小，但很多時候，正正就是在那個時刻，家人才真正重新成為一家人。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say it is still very important to have a meal together
with your family in Hong Kong, even if it cannot happen all the time. A shared
meal may look small from the outside, but quite often it is where a family
becomes a family again.
[總結來說，我會說在香港，和家人一起吃飯仍然是很重要的，即使那未必能夠時時刻刻發
生。一頓共同進餐從外表看來也許很小，但很多時候，正正就是在那個時刻，家人才真正重
新成為一家人。]

question.english: Is food now better than in the past?
question.chinese: 食物現在比以前更好嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is food now better than in the past?
食物現在比以前更好嗎？

components.english: If I had to choose, I’d say yes, food nowadays is generally better than it was in the past, mainly because people have far more choice and convenience.
components.chinese: 如果一定要我選，我會說是的，整體來看，現在的食物普遍比以前更好，主要因為人們現在有多得多的選擇，也方便得多。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose, I’d say yes, food nowadays is generally better than it
was in the past, mainly because people have far more choice and convenience.
[如果一定要我選，我會說是的，整體來看，現在的食物普遍比以前更好，主要因為人們現
在有多得多的選擇，也方便得多。]

components.english: Years ago, many people simply had to make do with whatever was local, seasonal, or affordable. Now, food from different cultures is much easier to find, and people can choose what suits their taste, budget, or lifestyle. On top of that, modern transport, refrigeration, and food delivery have made a big difference. In that sense, food today is not just about filling your stomach; it is also about variety, flexibility, and enjoyment.
components.chinese: 以前，很多人其實只能將就著吃當地、當季，或者自己負擔得起的食物。現在，不同文化的食物都更容易找到，人們也可以按照自己的口味、預算，或者生活方式去選擇。除此之外，現代運輸、冷藏技術，以及外送服務都帶來了很大的改變。從這個角度來看，今天的食物已經不只是用來填飽肚子；它同時也關乎多樣性、彈性，以及享受。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Years ago, many people simply had to make do with whatever
was local, seasonal, or affordable. Now, food from different cultures is much
easier to find, and people can choose what suits their taste, budget, or lifestyle.
On top of that, modern transport, refrigeration, and food delivery have made a
big difference. In that sense, food today is not just about filling your stomach; it
is also about variety, flexibility, and enjoyment.
[以前，很多人其實只能將就著吃當地、當季，或者自己負擔得起的食物。現在，不同文化的
食物都更容易找到，人們也可以按照自己的口味、預算，或者生活方式去選擇。除此之外，
現代運輸、冷藏技術，以及外送服務都帶來了很大的改變。從這個角度來看，今天的食物已
經不只是用來填飽肚子；它同時也關乎多樣性、彈性，以及享受。]

components.english: For example, these days someone can have Japanese food for lunch, Thai food for dinner, and then order fresh fruit or dessert straight to their doorstep. In the past, that kind of variety would have been much harder to come by.
components.chinese: 例如，現在一個人中午可以吃日本菜，晚上可以吃泰國菜，之後還可以直接把新鮮水果或甜品送到家門口。這種多樣性在以前其實是很難想像，或者至少沒有那麼容易得到的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, these days someone can have Japanese food for lunch,
Thai food for dinner, and then order fresh fruit or dessert straight to their
doorstep. In the past, that kind of variety would have been much harder to come
by.
[例如，現在一個人中午可以吃日本菜，晚上可以吃泰國菜，之後還可以直接把新鮮水果或
甜品送到家門口。這種多樣性在以前其實是很難想像，或者至少沒有那麼容易得到的。]

components.english: So, from that point of view, I would say food today is better, especially in terms of choice, access, and convenience.
components.chinese: 所以，從這個角度來看，我會說現在的食物的確更好，尤其是在選擇、取得方式，以及便利程度方面。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from that point of view, I would say food today is better,
especially in terms of choice, access, and convenience.
[所以，從這個角度來看，我會說現在的食物的確更好，尤其是在選擇、取得方式，以及便利
程度方面。]

components.english: That said, I do not think modern food is better across the board, because some of it is more processed, less wholesome, and a little too fast for its own good.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為現代食物在每一方面都比較好，因為其中有不少食物更加加工化、沒有那麼天然，而且某程度上也快得有點過了頭。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think modern food is better across the board,
because some of it is more processed, less wholesome, and a little too fast for its
own good.
[不過，我也不認為現代食物在每一方面都比較好，因為其中有不少食物更加加工化、沒有
那麼天然，而且某程度上也快得有點過了頭。]

components.english: To be honest, convenience can be a double-edged sword. Food is easier to get now, but it is not always healthier or more satisfying in the long run. In the past, meals were often simpler and more home-cooked, whereas now many people rely on takeaway, instant food, or highly processed snacks because life moves so fast. So while modern food may look more exciting on the surface, some of it has lost a bit of heart.
components.chinese: 老實說，便利本身就是一把雙刃劍。現在食物的確更容易得到，但長遠來看，它未必總是更健康，或者更能真正令人滿足。以前的飯菜通常比較簡單，也比較偏向家常；但現在很多人因為生活節奏太快，會更依賴外賣、即食食品，或者高度加工的小食。所以，雖然現代食物表面上看起來更吸引、更精彩，但其中有一部分其實也少了一點原本那種家的味道和溫度。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, convenience can be a double-edged sword. Food
is easier to get now, but it is not always healthier or more satisfying in the long
run. In the past, meals were often simpler and more home-cooked, whereas now
many people rely on takeaway, instant food, or highly processed snacks because
life moves so fast. So while modern food may look more exciting on the surface,
some of it has lost a bit of heart.
[老實說，便利本身就是一把雙刃劍。現在食物的確更容易得到，但長遠來看，它未必總是
更健康，或者更能真正令人滿足。以前的飯菜通常比較簡單，也比較偏向家常；但現在很多
人因為生活節奏太快，會更依賴外賣、即食食品，或者高度加工的小食。所以，雖然現代食
物表面上看起來更吸引、更精彩，但其中有一部分其實也少了一點原本那種家的味道和溫

components.english: For instance, a family in the past might have sat down to a simple home-cooked dinner made from fresh ingredients, while many people today eat in a rush, grab something on the go, or have dinner in front of a screen. The modern meal may save time, but it is not necessarily better for health or family connection.
components.chinese: 例如，以前一個家庭可能會一起坐下來，吃一頓用新鮮材料煮成的簡單家常飯；但今天很多人卻是一邊趕時間一邊吃、隨手買點東西充飢，或者一邊看著螢幕一邊吃晚飯。現代的用餐方式也許節省了時間，但未必對健康更好，也未必對家庭之間的連結更有幫助。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a family in the past might have sat down to a simple
home-cooked dinner made from fresh ingredients, while many people today eat
in a rush, grab something on the go, or have dinner in front of a screen. The
modern meal may save time, but it is not necessarily better for health or family
connection.
[例如，以前一個家庭可能會一起坐下來，吃一頓用新鮮材料煮成的簡單家常飯；但今天很
多人卻是一邊趕時間一邊吃、隨手買點東西充飢，或者一邊看著螢幕一邊吃晚飯。現代的用
餐方式也許節省了時間，但未必對健康更好，也未必對家庭之間的連結更有幫助。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say food now is better in some respects, but not in every respect. We have more choice and convenience than ever, but older food culture often had more freshness, more balance, and, in a way, a little more soul.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現在的食物在某些方面的確比以前更好，但並不是每一方面都更好。我們現在擁有前所未有的選擇和便利，但以往的飲食文化往往有更多新鮮感、更多平衡感，而且某程度上，也更有一點「靈魂」。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say food now is better in some respects, but not in
every respect. We have more choice and convenience than ever, but older food
culture often had more freshness, more balance, and, in a way, a little more soul.
[總結來說，我會說現在的食物在某些方面的確比以前更好，但並不是每一方面都更好。我
們現在擁有前所未有的選擇和便利，但以往的飲食文化往往有更多新鮮感、更多平衡感，而
且某程度上，也更有一點「靈魂」。]

question.english: What kind of people would like to go to a cafe?
question.chinese: 甚麼樣的人會喜歡去咖啡店呢？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kind of people would like to go to a cafe?
甚麼樣的人會喜歡去咖啡店呢？

components.english: I think cafés tend to attract people who want a change of scene, a bit of comfort, and a place where they can slow down without feeling cut off from the world.
components.chinese: 我認為，咖啡店通常會吸引那些想換一換環境、想要一點舒適感，同時又想在不與世界隔絕的情況下放慢節奏的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think cafés tend to attract people who want a change of scene, a bit of
comfort, and a place where they can slow down without feeling cut off from the
world.
[我認為，咖啡店通常會吸引那些想換一換環境、想要一點舒適感，同時又想在不與世界隔
絕的情況下放慢節奏的人。]

components.english: Some people go to cafés because they enjoy the atmosphere itself. It is often more relaxing than an office, but more lively than staying at home alone. People can chat, read, think, or simply watch the world go by. In that sense, a café can feel like a small pocket of breathing space in the middle of a busy day.
components.chinese: 有些人去咖啡店，單純就是因為喜歡那種氣氛。它通常比辦公室更放鬆，但又比一個人待在家裡更有生氣。人們可以聊天、看書、思考，或者只是靜靜看著外面的世界流動。從這個角度來看，咖啡店就像繁忙一天中的一小塊喘息空間。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Some people go to cafés because they enjoy the atmosphere itself.
It is often more relaxing than an office, but more lively than staying at home
alone. People can chat, read, think, or simply watch the world go by. In that
sense, a café can feel like a small pocket of breathing space in the middle of a
busy day.
[有些人去咖啡店，單純就是因為喜歡那種氣氛。它通常比辦公室更放鬆，但又比一個人待
在家裡更有生氣。人們可以聊天、看書、思考，或者只是靜靜看著外面的世界流動。從這個
角度來看，咖啡店就像繁忙一天中的一小塊喘息空間。]

components.english: For example, office workers may drop by after a stressful morning to clear their head, friends may meet there to catch up, and some people may just sit by the window with a drink and enjoy a little quiet time without complete silence.
components.chinese: 例如，上班族可能會在忙亂的早上之後去咖啡店清一清腦袋；朋友們可能會約在那裡聚一聚；也有些人只是想坐在窗邊喝杯飲品，在不完全安靜的環境裡享受片刻寧靜。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, office workers may drop by after a stressful morning
to clear their head, friends may meet there to catch up, and some people may
just sit by the window with a drink and enjoy a little quiet time without
complete silence.
[例如，上班族可能會在忙亂的早上之後去咖啡店清一清腦袋；朋友們可能會約在那裡聚一
聚；也有些人只是想坐在窗邊喝杯飲品，在不完全安靜的環境裡享受片刻寧靜。]

components.english: So, one kind of people who like going to cafés are those who want comfort, company, and a gentle break from routine.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一類喜歡去咖啡店的人，就是那些想要舒適感、陪伴感，以及從日常節奏中暫時抽身的人。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one kind of people who like going to cafés are those who
want comfort, company, and a gentle break from routine.
[所以，其中一類喜歡去咖啡店的人，就是那些想要舒適感、陪伴感，以及從日常節奏中暫
時抽身的人。]

components.english: Another major group would be students, freelancers, and people who like to work or study in a relaxed public space.
components.chinese: 另一大類人，會是學生、自由工作者，以及那些喜歡在輕鬆公共空間裡工作或溫習的人。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another major group would be students, freelancers, and people who
like to work or study in a relaxed public space.
[另一大類人，會是學生、自由工作者，以及那些喜歡在輕鬆公共空間裡工作或溫習的人。]

components.english: To be honest, some people focus better with a little background buzz than in total silence. Home can be full of distractions, whereas a café gives them just enough structure to get the ball rolling. It is not as formal as a library and not as demanding as a workplace, so for some people it hits the sweet spot.
components.chinese: 老實說，有些人在有一點背景聲的環境裡，反而比在完全安靜的地方更容易專心。家裡往往充滿各種干擾；相反，咖啡店卻能給他們剛剛好的外在節奏，幫助他們開始進入狀態。它沒有圖書館那麼正式，也沒有工作場所那麼有壓迫感，所以對某些人來說，它正好落在最舒服的平衡點上。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, some people focus better with a little
background buzz than in total silence. Home can be full of distractions, whereas
a café gives them just enough structure to get the ball rolling. It is not as formal
as a library and not as demanding as a workplace, so for some people it hits the
sweet spot.
[老實說，有些人在有一點背景聲的環境裡，反而比在完全安靜的地方更容易專心。家裡往
往充滿各種干擾；相反，咖啡店卻能給他們剛剛好的外在節奏，幫助他們開始進入狀態。它
沒有圖書館那麼正式，也沒有工作場所那麼有壓迫感，所以對某些人來說，它正好落在最舒
服的平衡點上。]

components.english: For instance, a university student may revise for an exam in a café for two hours, or a freelance designer may bring a laptop there to work through ideas over coffee. In both cases, the café is not just selling drinks; it is offering an environment that helps people settle in and get things done.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大學生可能會在咖啡店裡溫習兩小時準備考試；一位自由身設計師也可能帶著手提電腦去那裡，一邊喝咖啡一邊整理靈感。在這兩種情況下，咖啡店賣的不只是飲品，而是一種能幫助人安定下來、把事情做好 的環境。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a university student may revise for an exam in a café
for two hours, or a freelance designer may bring a laptop there to work through
ideas over coffee. In both cases, the café is not just selling drinks; it is offering an
environment that helps people settle in and get things done.
[例如，一個大學生可能會在咖啡店裡溫習兩小時準備考試；一位自由身設計師也可能帶著
手提電腦去那裡，一邊喝咖啡一邊整理靈感。在這兩種情況下，咖啡店賣的不只是飲品，而
是一種能幫助人安定下來、把事情做好 的環境。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say cafés appeal to a wide range of people, from those looking for relaxation and social connection to those wanting a calm but lively place to work, study, or simply recharge.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說咖啡店吸引的人其實很廣，從想放鬆、想和人連結的人，到那些想找一個平靜但不死寂的地方工作、溫習，或者替自己重新充電的人，都可能會喜歡去咖啡店。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say cafés appeal to a wide range of people, from
those looking for relaxation and social connection to those wanting a calm but
lively place to work, study, or simply recharge.
[總結來說，我會說咖啡店吸引的人其實很廣，從想放鬆、想和人連結的人，到那些想找一
個平靜但不死寂的地方工作、溫習，或者替自己重新充電的人，都可能會喜歡去咖啡店。]

question.english: Why do young people like studying in a café instead of at home?
question.chinese: 主題：為甚麼年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，而不是在家裡？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do young people like studying in a café instead of at home?
主題：為甚麼年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，而不是在家裡？

components.english: I think many young people like studying in a café because it helps them feel more focused and less distracted than they might be at home.
components.chinese: 我認為，很多年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，是因為那樣往往能讓他們更專注，也比在家裡時更不容易分心。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think many young people like studying in a café because it helps them
feel more focused and less distracted than they might be at home.
[我認為，很多年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，是因為那樣往往能讓他們更專注，也比在家裡時
更不容易分心。]

components.english: The main reason is that home is often full of temptations. When people study at home, it is very easy to drift towards the sofa, the bed, the fridge, or their phone. A café, by contrast, creates a kind of middle ground: it is public enough to keep people alert, but still relaxed enough to feel comfortable. In that sense, it gives them a clearer line between “rest mode” and “study mode”. Sometimes a change of scenery is half the battle.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，家裡往往充滿各種令人分心的誘惑。當人在家讀書時，很容易不知不覺就走向梳化、床、雪櫃，或者手機。相比之下，咖啡店提供了一種介乎兩者之間的空間：它夠公開，所以能讓人保持清醒；但同時又夠輕鬆，不會令人太有壓力。從這個角度來看，它能幫助人更清楚地劃分「休息模式」和「讀書模式」。很多時候，換一個環境，本身就已經成功了一半。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that home is often full of temptations. When
people study at home, it is very easy to drift towards the sofa, the bed, the
fridge, or their phone. A café, by contrast, creates a kind of middle ground: it is
public enough to keep people alert, but still relaxed enough to feel comfortable.
In that sense, it gives them a clearer line between “rest mode” and “study mode”.
Sometimes a change of scenery is half the battle.
[主要原因是，家裡往往充滿各種令人分心的誘惑。當人在家讀書時，很容易不知不覺就走
向梳化、床、雪櫃，或者手機。相比之下，咖啡店提供了一種介乎兩者之間的空間：它夠公開
，所以能讓人保持清醒；但同時又夠輕鬆，不會令人太有壓力。從這個角度來看，它能幫助
人更清楚地劃分「休息模式」和「讀書模式」。很多時候，換一個環境，本身就已經成功了一

components.english: For example, a university student who tries to revise at home may end up lying on the bed, checking social media, or wandering into the kitchen every twenty minutes. However, if the same person sits in a café with a laptop, a drink, and a few hours set aside, they are much more likely to settle down and actually get some work done.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大學生如果在家溫習，可能最後會變成躺在床上、刷社交媒體，或者每二十分鐘就走進廚房一次。不過，如果同一個人坐在咖啡店裡，帶着電腦、點一杯飲品，再留出幾個小時讀書，他通常就更有可能安定下來，真正完成一些事情。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a university student who tries to revise at home may
end up lying on the bed, checking social media, or wandering into the kitchen
every twenty minutes. However, if the same person sits in a café with a laptop, a
drink, and a few hours set aside, they are much more likely to settle down and
actually get some work done.
[例如，一個大學生如果在家溫習，可能最後會變成躺在床上、刷社交媒體，或者每二十分
鐘就走進廚房一次。不過，如果同一個人坐在咖啡店裡，帶着電腦、點一杯飲品，再留出幾
個小時讀書，他通常就更有可能安定下來，真正完成一些事情。]

components.english: So, I’d say one big reason young people study in cafés is that the environment makes concentration feel easier and procrastination a little harder.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說，年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，其中一個很大的原因，就是那種環境會讓專注變得比較容易，也讓拖延變得沒那麼順手。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I’d say one big reason young people study in cafés is that the
environment makes concentration feel easier and procrastination a little harder.
[所以，我會說，年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，其中一個很大的原因，就是那種環境會讓專注
變得比較容易，也讓拖延變得沒那麼順手。]

components.english: On top of that, many young people enjoy the atmosphere of a café, because it can make studying feel less lonely, less dull, and a bit more enjoyable.
components.chinese: 除此之外，很多年輕人也很喜歡咖啡店的氣氛，因為那會讓讀書這件事感覺起來沒那麼孤單、沒那麼沉悶，也稍微更有趣一點。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, many young people enjoy the atmosphere of a café,
because it can make studying feel less lonely, less dull, and a bit more enjoyable.
[除此之外，很多年輕人也很喜歡咖啡店的氣氛，因為那會讓讀書這件事感覺起來沒那麼孤
單、沒那麼沉悶，也稍微更有趣一點。]

components.english: To be honest, some people work better with a gentle buzz in the background rather than total silence. The sound of cups clinking, coffee machines humming, and people talking softly can create a rhythm that feels oddly comforting. It is almost like borrowing a bit of energy from the room. Also, studying in a café can feel more intentional, even a little aesthetic, which makes it easier for some young people to get into the zone.
components.chinese: 老實說，有些人反而在有一點背景聲的情況下工作得更好，而不是在完全安靜的環境裡。杯子碰撞的聲音、咖啡機運作的嗡嗡聲，以及人們低聲交談的聲音，往往會形成一種令人意外地感到舒服的節奏。那感覺幾乎像是在向整個空間借一點能量。另外，在咖啡店讀書也會給人一種更有儀式感，甚至稍微更有氛圍的感覺，而這對一些年輕人來說，的確更容易進入狀態。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, some people work better with a gentle buzz in
the background rather than total silence. The sound of cups clinking, coffee
machines humming, and people talking softly can create a rhythm that feels
oddly comforting. It is almost like borrowing a bit of energy from the room.
Also, studying in a café can feel more intentional, even a little aesthetic, which
makes it easier for some young people to get into the zone.
[老實說，有些人反而在有一點背景聲的情況下工作得更好，而不是在完全安靜的環境裡。
杯子碰撞的聲音、咖啡機運作的嗡嗡聲，以及人們低聲交談的聲音，往往會形成一種令人意
外地感到舒服的節奏。那感覺幾乎像是在向整個空間借一點能量。另外，在咖啡店讀書也會
給人一種更有儀式感，甚至稍微更有氛圍的感覺，而這對一些年輕人來說，的確更容易進入
狀態。]

components.english: For instance, some students like to meet a friend in a café and study side by side without talking much. Even when they are working on different things, that shared setting can keep them motivated. It is a bit like saying, “We’re all here to get our heads down,” and that quiet sense of accountability can really help.
components.chinese: 例如，有些學生喜歡和朋友約在咖啡店裡，各自安靜地讀書，就算彼此沒怎麼聊天也沒關係。即使大家做的是不同的事情，那種一起坐着努力的感覺仍然能帶來推動力。那有點像在默默地說：「我們都是來這裡認真做事的。」而這種安靜的互相督促，很多時確實很有幫助。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some students like to meet a friend in a café and study
side by side without talking much. Even when they are working on different
things, that shared setting can keep them motivated. It is a bit like saying,
“We’re all here to get our heads down,” and that quiet sense of accountability
can really help.
[例如，有些學生喜歡和朋友約在咖啡店裡，各自安靜地讀書，就算彼此沒怎麼聊天也沒關
係。即使大家做的是不同的事情，那種一起坐着努力的感覺仍然能帶來推動力。那有點像在
默默地說：「我們都是來這裡認真做事的。」而這種安靜的互相督促，很多時確實很有幫助。]

components.english: Overall, I think young people like studying in cafés because they offer a better balance between structure and comfort. It may not be the perfect place for everyone, but for many people, it turns studying from something heavy and dull into something lighter, steadier, and easier to stick with.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，是因為那裡往往能在規律感與舒適感之間取得更好的平衡。它未必對每一個人都是最完美的地方，但對很多人來說，它的確能把原本沉重而枯燥的讀書過程，變得比較輕盈、比較穩定，也比較容易堅持下去。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think young people like studying in cafés because
they offer a better balance between structure and comfort. It may not be the
perfect place for everyone, but for many people, it turns studying from
something heavy and dull into something lighter, steadier, and easier to stick
with.
[總結來說，我認為年輕人喜歡在咖啡店讀書，是因為那裡往往能在規律感與舒適感之間取
得更好的平衡。它未必對每一個人都是最完美的地方，但對很多人來說，它的確能把原本沉
重而枯燥的讀書過程，變得比較輕盈、比較穩定，也比較容易堅持下去。]

question.english: Do older people like to drink coffee?
question.chinese: 主題：年長的人喜歡喝咖啡嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do older people like to drink coffee?
主題：年長的人喜歡喝咖啡嗎？

components.english: I would say many older people do like to drink coffee, but it really depends on personal habit, taste, and lifestyle.
components.chinese: 我會說，很多年長的人確實喜歡喝咖啡，不過這其實很視乎個人習慣、口味，以及生活方式。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I would say many older people do like to drink coffee, but it really
depends on personal habit, taste, and lifestyle.
[我會說，很多年長的人確實喜歡喝咖啡，不過這其實很視乎個人習慣、口味，以及生活方

components.english: For some older people, coffee is more than just a drink; it is part of the rhythm of the day. It can be a morning ritual, a small comfort, or a way to feel more awake and focused. If someone has been drinking coffee for years, they may see it as second nature. In that sense, a cup of coffee can feel less like a luxury and more like an old friend.
components.chinese: 對某些年長的人來說，咖啡不只是一杯飲品；它其實是日常節奏的一部分。它可以是一種早晨儀式、一點小小的安慰，或者一種讓自己更清醒、更專注的方法。如果一個人已經喝了很多年咖啡，那對他來說往往會變得非常自然。從這個角度來看，一杯咖啡與其說像奢侈享受，倒不如說更像一位老朋友。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: For some older people, coffee is more than just a drink; it is part
of the rhythm of the day. It can be a morning ritual, a small comfort, or a way to
feel more awake and focused. If someone has been drinking coffee for years, they
may see it as second nature. In that sense, a cup of coffee can feel less like a
luxury and more like an old friend.
[對某些年長的人來說，咖啡不只是一杯飲品；它其實是日常節奏的一部分。它可以是一種
早晨儀式、一點小小的安慰，或者一種讓自己更清醒、更專注的方法。如果一個人已經喝了
很多年咖啡，那對他來說往往會變得非常自然。從這個角度來看，一杯咖啡與其說像奢侈享
受，倒不如說更像一位老朋友。]

components.english: For example, an older person may enjoy sitting down in the morning with a newspaper, a quiet breakfast, and a cup of coffee before the day properly begins. That simple routine can be deeply comforting.
components.chinese: 例如，一位年長的人可能很喜歡在早上坐下來，一邊看報紙、一邊吃簡單早餐，再配上一杯咖啡，讓一天慢慢展開。這種看似簡單的習慣，其實可以帶來很深的安定感。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, an older person may enjoy sitting down in the
morning with a newspaper, a quiet breakfast, and a cup of coffee before the day
properly begins. That simple routine can be deeply comforting.
[例如，一位年長的人可能很喜歡在早上坐下來，一邊看報紙、一邊吃簡單早餐，再配上一
杯咖啡，讓一天慢慢展開。這種看似簡單的習慣，其實可以帶來很深的安定感。]

components.english: So yes, many older people do like coffee, especially when it has become part of their daily routine and sense of comfort.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，很多年長的人確實喜歡咖啡，尤其是當它已經成為他們日常習慣和舒適感來源的一部分時。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, many older people do like coffee, especially when it has
become part of their daily routine and sense of comfort.
[所以，是的，很多年長的人確實喜歡咖啡，尤其是當它已經成為他們日常習慣和舒適感來
源的一部分時。]

components.english: That said, not all older people enjoy coffee, and many may actually prefer tea or avoid caffeine altogether.
components.chinese: 不過，並不是所有年長的人都喜歡咖啡，而且也有很多人其實會比較喜歡茶，或者乾脆避免攝取咖啡因。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, not all older people enjoy coffee, and many may actually
prefer tea or avoid caffeine altogether.
[不過，並不是所有年長的人都喜歡咖啡，而且也有很多人其實會比較喜歡茶，或者乾脆避
免攝取咖啡因。]

components.english: As people get older, they may become more sensitive to caffeine, so coffee can start to interfere with sleep, upset the stomach, or make them feel a little on edge. On top of that, drinking habits are often shaped by culture and generation. In some families, tea is the drink that truly hits the spot, whereas coffee may feel too bitter, too strong, or simply not their cup of tea, quite literally. So, age matters a little, but preference and health matter even more.
components.chinese: 隨著年紀漸長，人有時會對咖啡因變得更敏感，所以咖啡可能會開始影響睡眠、刺激腸胃，或者令人覺得有點緊繃。除此之外，飲食習慣往往也深受文化和世代影響。在某些家庭裡，真正最合心意的始終是茶；而咖啡則可能顯得太苦、太濃，或者根本不是他們喜歡的那一味。由此可見，年齡的確有一點影響，但真正更重要的，還是個人口味和身體狀況。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: As people get older, they may become more sensitive to
caffeine, so coffee can start to interfere with sleep, upset the stomach, or make
them feel a little on edge. On top of that, drinking habits are often shaped by
culture and generation. In some families, tea is the drink that truly hits the
spot, whereas coffee may feel too bitter, too strong, or simply not their cup of
tea, quite literally. So, age matters a little, but preference and health matter even
more.
[隨著年紀漸長，人有時會對咖啡因變得更敏感，所以咖啡可能會開始影響睡眠、刺激腸胃
，或者令人覺得有點緊繃。除此之外，飲食習慣往往也深受文化和世代影響。在某些家庭裡
，真正最合心意的始終是茶；而咖啡則可能顯得太苦、太濃，或者根本不是他們喜歡的那一
味。由此可見，年齡的確有一點影響，但真正更重要的，還是個人口味和身體狀況。]

components.english: For instance, an older woman who used to drink coffee every afternoon may gradually switch to tea after finding that coffee keeps her awake at night. In that case, the change is not about dislike; it is more about what suits her body better now.
components.chinese: 例如，一位本來每天下午都喝咖啡的年長女士，後來可能會慢慢轉喝茶，因為她發現咖啡會讓自己晚上睡不著。在這種情況下，那種改變未必是因為她不再喜歡咖啡，而更多是因為現在有另一種飲品更適合她的身體。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, an older woman who used to drink coffee every
afternoon may gradually switch to tea after finding that coffee keeps her awake
at night. In that case, the change is not about dislike; it is more about what suits
her body better now.
[例如，一位本來每天下午都喝咖啡的年長女士，後來可能會慢慢轉喝茶，因為她發現咖啡
會讓自己晚上睡不著。在這種情況下，那種改變未必是因為她不再喜歡咖啡，而更多是因為
現在有另一種飲品更適合她的身體。]

components.english: Overall, I would say some older people really enjoy coffee, while others do not care for it at all. In the end, it is less about age itself and more about habit, health, and what brings a person comfort in daily life.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說有些年長的人真的很喜歡咖啡，而另一些人則完全不太喜歡。說到底，這與其說是年齡本身造成的，不如說更多取決於習慣、健康狀況，以及甚麼東西能在日常生活中帶給一個人舒適感。

question.english: Do people in your country like to drink coffee?
question.chinese: 香港人喜歡喝咖啡嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do people in your country like to drink coffee?
香港人喜歡喝咖啡嗎？

components.english: Yes, I would say many Hong Kong people do like to drink coffee, especially younger people, office workers, and those who live a fast-paced lifestyle.
components.chinese: 是的，我會說很多香港人的確喜歡喝咖啡，尤其是年輕人、上班族，以及那些生活節奏很快的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I would say many Hong Kong people do like to drink coffee,
especially younger people, office workers, and those who live a fast-paced
lifestyle.
[是的，我會說很多香港人的確喜歡喝咖啡，尤其是年輕人、上班族，以及那些生活節奏很
快的人。]

components.english: The main reason is that coffee fits the rhythm of the city quite well. Hong Kong is busy, crowded, and always on the move, so coffee often becomes more than just a drink. For many people, it is a morning ritual, a quick pick-me-up, or simply fuel in the tank before a long working day. On top of that, café culture has grown a lot, so drinking coffee is not only about staying awake; it is also about relaxing, catching up with friends, or getting a bit of breathing space in the middle of a hectic day.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，咖啡和這座城市的節奏其實非常合拍。香港生活忙碌、擠迫，而且總是在趕路，所以咖啡很多時已經不只是一杯飲品。對不少人來說，它是一種早晨儀式、一個快速提神的方法，或者只是漫長工作天開始之前替自己加一點油。除此之外，咖啡店文化也發展得很快，所以喝咖啡不只是為了保持清醒；它同時也可以是放鬆一下、和朋友聊天，或者在繁忙日子中偷回一點喘息空間。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that coffee fits the rhythm of the city quite
well. Hong Kong is busy, crowded, and always on the move, so coffee often
becomes more than just a drink. For many people, it is a morning ritual, a quick
pick-me-up, or simply fuel in the tank before a long working day. On top of
that, café culture has grown a lot, so drinking coffee is not only about staying
awake; it is also about relaxing, catching up with friends, or getting a bit of
breathing space in the middle of a hectic day.
[主要原因是，咖啡和這座城市的節奏其實非常合拍。香港生活忙碌、擠迫，而且總是在趕
路，所以咖啡很多時已經不只是一杯飲品。對不少人來說，它是一種早晨儀式、一個快速提
神的方法，或者只是漫長工作天開始之前替自己加一點油。除此之外，咖啡店文化也發展得
很快，所以喝咖啡不只是為了保持清醒；它同時也可以是放鬆一下、和朋友聊天，或者在繁
忙日子中偷回一點喘息空間。]

components.english: For example, it is very common to see office workers grabbing a takeaway coffee on the way to work, or students sitting in cafés with an iced latte while studying. Specialty coffee shops have also become much more popular, so some people now care not just about caffeine, but also about flavour, beans, and the whole café experience.
components.chinese: 例如，你很常會看見上班族在上班途中買一杯外賣咖啡，或者學生坐在咖啡店裡，一邊喝凍拿鐵一邊溫習。精品咖啡店近年也變得越來越受歡迎，所以有些人現在在意的已經不只是咖啡因，還包括味道、咖啡豆，以及整個咖啡店體驗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, it is very common to see office workers grabbing a
takeaway coffee on the way to work, or students sitting in cafés with an iced
latte while studying. Specialty coffee shops have also become much more
popular, so some people now care not just about caffeine, but also about flavour,
beans, and the whole café experience.
[例如，你很常會看見上班族在上班途中買一杯外賣咖啡，或者學生坐在咖啡店裡，一邊喝
凍拿鐵一邊溫習。精品咖啡店近年也變得越來越受歡迎，所以有些人現在在意的已經不只
是咖啡因，還包括味道、咖啡豆，以及整個咖啡店體驗。]

components.english: So yes, I do think coffee is very popular in Hong Kong, because it matches both the pace and the lifestyle of the city.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為咖啡在香港很受歡迎，因為它和這座城市的步伐，以及人們的生活方式都很配合。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think coffee is very popular in Hong Kong, because it
matches both the pace and the lifestyle of the city.
[所以，是的，我的確認為咖啡在香港很受歡迎，因為它和這座城市的步伐，以及人們的生
活方式都很配合。]

components.english: That said, I would not say Hong Kong is a coffee-only culture, because traditional drinks still have a very strong place here.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說香港是一個只喝咖啡的地方，因為傳統飲品在這裡仍然有非常穩固的位置。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say Hong Kong is a coffee-only culture, because
traditional drinks still have a very strong place here.
[不過，我也不會說香港是一個只喝咖啡的地方，因為傳統飲品在這裡仍然有非常穩固的位

components.english: To be honest, Hong Kong people often enjoy both worlds. Coffee is popular, but so are milk tea, lemon tea, and other cha chaan teng classics. For many locals, it is not really a question of coffee or tea; it is more about mood, habit, and occasion. Coffee may suit a rushed morning or a café meet-up, whereas Hong Kong-style milk tea carries a more familiar, nostalgic kind of comfort. In other words, coffee has found a home here, but it has not pushed the old favourites off the table.
components.chinese: 老實說，香港人很多時候其實是兩邊都喜歡。咖啡很受歡迎，但奶茶、檸檬茶，以及其他茶餐廳經典飲品也同樣受歡迎。對很多本地人來說，這並不真的是咖啡和茶二選一的問題，而更取決於心情、習慣，以及場合。咖啡也許適合忙碌的早晨，或者在咖啡店見面時喝；而港式奶茶則帶來一種更熟悉、更有懷舊味道的安慰感。換句話說，咖啡在香港的確站穩了腳，但它並沒有把那些老朋友擠下桌。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, Hong Kong people often enjoy both worlds.

classics. For many locals, it is not really a question of coffee or tea; it is more

更取決於心情、習慣，以及場合。咖啡也許適合忙碌的早晨，或者在咖啡店見面時喝；而港

但它並沒有把那些老朋友擠下桌。]

components.english: For instance, someone might have a cappuccino during the workweek, then go to a cha chaan teng at the weekend and order hot milk tea without even thinking twice. That is actually quite typical in Hong Kong. People are open to international tastes, but they still keep one foot firmly in local food culture.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人平日上班時可能會喝一杯泡沫咖啡，但到了週末去茶餐廳時，又會很自然地點一杯熱奶茶。這其實在香港是很典型的情況。人們對國際口味很開放，但同時也仍然穩穩地站在本地飲食文化之中。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone might have a cappuccino during the
workweek, then go to a cha chaan teng at the weekend and order hot milk tea
without even thinking twice. That is actually quite typical in Hong Kong.
People are open to international tastes, but they still keep one foot firmly in

components.english: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong people do like coffee quite a lot, but they do not love it at the expense of everything else. Coffee is popular, certainly, but it shares the stage with older and deeply rooted local drinks.

咖啡當然很受歡迎，不過它仍然是和那些更傳統、也更深植本地文化的飲品一起並存的。]

question.english: Does the taste of coffee vary by country?
question.chinese: 主題：咖啡的味道會因國家而不同嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Does the taste of coffee vary by country?
[主題：咖啡的味道會因國家而不同嗎？]

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think the taste of coffee can vary quite a lot from country to country.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對會。我認為咖啡的味道的確會因不同國家而有相當大的差異。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think the taste of coffee can vary quite a lot from
country to country.
[是的，絕對會。我認為咖啡的味道的確會因不同國家而有相當大的差異。]

components.english: The main reason is that coffee is shaped by more than just the beans themselves. Climate, soil, roasting style, brewing method, and even local drinking habits all leave their mark on the final cup. In that sense, coffee is a bit like language: the basic thing may be the same, but each country speaks it with a different accent. Some places prefer coffee that is dark, bold, and intense, while others go for something smoother, lighter, or sweeter.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，咖啡的風味並不只是由咖啡豆本身決定。氣候、土壤、烘焙方式、沖煮方法，甚至當地人的飲用習慣，都會在最後那杯咖啡裡留下痕跡。從這個角度來說，咖啡有點像語言：基本上是同一樣東西，但每個國家都會用不同的「口音」去表達。有些地方偏好深沉、濃烈、衝擊感強的咖啡；而另一些地方則較喜歡順口、清淡，或者偏甜的風味。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that coffee is shaped by more than just the
beans themselves. Climate, soil, roasting style, brewing method, and even local
drinking habits all leave their mark on the final cup. In that sense, coffee is a bit
like language: the basic thing may be the same, but each country speaks it with a
different accent. Some places prefer coffee that is dark, bold, and intense, while
others go for something smoother, lighter, or sweeter.
[主要原因是，咖啡的風味並不只是由咖啡豆本身決定。氣候、土壤、烘焙方式、沖煮方法，
甚至當地人的飲用習慣，都會在最後那杯咖啡裡留下痕跡。從這個角度來說，咖啡有點像語
言：基本上是同一樣東西，但每個國家都會用不同的「口音」去表達。有些地方偏好深沉、濃
烈、衝擊感強的咖啡；而另一些地方則較喜歡順口、清淡，或者偏甜的風味。]

components.english: For example, Italian coffee is often associated with short, strong espresso, whereas Turkish coffee tends to be thicker and more intense. By contrast, in some places people may enjoy coffee with milk, sugar, or condensed milk, which creates a completely different experience. So even if the drink is still called coffee, the flavour profile can feel worlds apart.
components.chinese: 例如，意大利咖啡通常會令人想到短小而濃烈的 espresso，而土耳其咖啡則往往更厚重，也更強烈。相比之下，在某些地方，人們會喜歡加入牛奶、糖，甚至煉奶，這樣就會帶來完全不同的體驗。所以，即使那杯飲品仍然叫做咖啡，它的風味輪廓卻可能相差很遠。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, Italian coffee is often associated with short, strong
espresso, whereas Turkish coffee tends to be thicker and more intense. By
contrast, in some places people may enjoy coffee with milk, sugar, or condensed
milk, which creates a completely different experience. So even if the drink is still
called coffee, the flavour profile can feel worlds apart.
[例如，意大利咖啡通常會令人想到短小而濃烈的 espresso，而土耳其咖啡則往往更厚重，
也更強烈。相比之下，在某些地方，人們會喜歡加入牛奶、糖，甚至煉奶，這樣就會帶來完全
不同的體驗。所以，即使那杯飲品仍然叫做咖啡，它的風味輪廓卻可能相差很遠。]

components.english: So yes, I would definitely say coffee tastes different across countries, because each place puts its own fingerprint on the cup.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我一定會說咖啡在不同國家的味道確實不同，因為每個地方都會在那杯咖啡上留下自己的指紋。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would definitely say coffee tastes different across
countries, because each place puts its own fingerprint on the cup.
[所以，是的，我一定會說咖啡在不同國家的味道確實不同，因為每個地方都會在那杯咖啡
上留下自己的指紋。]

components.english: That said, I do think the differences are not always as dramatic as they used to be, because coffee culture has become much more global.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，這些差異未必總像以前那麼明顯，因為咖啡文化已經變得愈來愈全球化。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think the differences are not always as dramatic as they
used to be, because coffee culture has become much more global.
[不過，我也認為，這些差異未必總像以前那麼明顯，因為咖啡文化已經變得愈來愈全球

components.english: These days, people can find international coffee chains, imported beans, and similar café trends almost everywhere, so some tastes have become more standardised. A latte in one big city may not be wildly different from a latte in another. Still, local preference usually has the final say. Even when the menu looks familiar, the way coffee is roasted, served, and enjoyed can still reflect the character of the country. In other words, the cup may travel, but taste still has roots.
components.chinese: 如今，人們在很多地方都能找到國際連鎖咖啡店、進口咖啡豆，以及相似的咖啡店潮流，所以某些味道的確已經變得比較標準化。在一個大城市喝到的 latte，未必會和另一個大城市的 latte 差得很誇張。不過，當地人的口味偏好通常還是最後的決定因素。即使菜單看起來很熟悉，咖啡實際上如何烘焙、如何端上來，以及如何被享受，仍然會反映那個國家的性格。換句話說，杯子可以四處走，但味道仍然有根。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, people can find international coffee chains,
imported beans, and similar café trends almost everywhere, so some tastes have
become more standardised. A latte in one big city may not be wildly different
from a latte in another. Still, local preference usually has the final say. Even
when the menu looks familiar, the way coffee is roasted, served, and enjoyed can
still reflect the character of the country. In other words, the cup may travel, but
taste still has roots.
[如今，人們在很多地方都能找到國際連鎖咖啡店、進口咖啡豆，以及相似的咖啡店潮流，
所以某些味道的確已經變得比較標準化。在一個大城市喝到的 latte，未必會和另一個大城
市的 latte 差得很誇張。不過，當地人的口味偏好通常還是最後的決定因素。即使菜單看起
來很熟悉，咖啡實際上如何烘焙、如何端上來，以及如何被享受，仍然會反映那個國家的性
格。換句話說，杯子可以四處走，但味道仍然有根。]

components.english: For instance, two cafés in different countries may both sell cappuccino, but one version may be stronger and less sweet, while the other may be softer, creamier, or more dessert-like. So the difference may sometimes be subtle rather than dramatic, but it is still there if you pay attention.
components.chinese: 例如，不同國家的兩間咖啡店都可能賣 cappuccino，但其中一杯可能更濃、更不甜；另一杯則可能更柔和、更綿密，甚至更像甜品。所以，那種差別有時未必是很戲劇化的大不同，而是較細微的變化；不過，只要你留心，其實還是感覺得到。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, two cafés in different countries may both sell
cappuccino, but one version may be stronger and less sweet, while the other may
be softer, creamier, or more dessert-like. So the difference may sometimes be
subtle rather than dramatic, but it is still there if you pay attention.
[例如，不同國家的兩間咖啡店都可能賣 cappuccino，但其中一杯可能更濃、更不甜；另一杯
則可能更柔和、更綿密，甚至更像甜品。所以，那種差別有時未必是很戲劇化的大不同，而
是較細微的變化；不過，只要你留心，其實還是感覺得到。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the taste of coffee does vary by country, even if globalisation has blurred the edges a little. Coffee may be a global drink, but it still carries a local soul.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說咖啡的味道確實會因國家而不同，即使全球化已經把那些界線稍微模糊了一點。咖啡也許是一種全球性的飲品，但它仍然帶著很在地的靈魂。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the taste of coffee does vary by country, even if
globalisation has blurred the edges a little. Coffee may be a global drink, but it
still carries a local soul.
[總結來說，我會說咖啡的味道確實會因國家而不同，即使全球化已經把那些界線稍微模糊
了一點。咖啡也許是一種全球性的飲品，但它仍然帶著很在地的靈魂。]

question.english: Would you like to have a few very good friends or a lot of just friends?
question.chinese: 你會想擁有幾個很好的朋友，還是很多普通朋友？

source_lines.text: Topic: Would you like to have a few very good friends or a lot of just friends?
你會想擁有幾個很好的朋友，還是很多普通朋友？

components.english: Personally, I’d much rather have a few very good friends than a large number of just friends.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我會寧願擁有幾個很好的朋友，而不是一大群普通朋友。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I’d much rather have a few very good friends than a large
number of just friends.
[就我個人而言，我會寧願擁有幾個很好的朋友，而不是一大群普通朋友。]

components.english: The main reason is that deep friendships usually bring trust, honesty, and real emotional support. When life is smooth, almost anyone can share a laugh with you. But when the chips are down, it is usually a small circle that truly shows up. To me, friendship is less about numbers and more about depth. A few close friends can feel like solid ground in a world that sometimes shifts under your feet.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，深厚的友誼通常會帶來信任、坦誠，以及真正的情感支持。當生活一切順利時，幾乎任何人都可以和你一起笑一笑；但當你真的遇到困難時，往往只有那一小圈真正親近的人才會站出來。對我來說，友情不太是關於數量，而更是關於深度。幾個親密朋友，有時就像在不太穩定的世界裡，給你一塊踏實的地。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that deep friendships usually bring trust,
honesty, and real emotional support. When life is smooth, almost anyone can
share a laugh with you. But when the chips are down, it is usually a small circle
that truly shows up. To me, friendship is less about numbers and more about
depth. A few close friends can feel like solid ground in a world that sometimes
shifts under your feet.
[主要原因是，深厚的友誼通常會帶來信任、坦誠，以及真正的情感支持。當生活一切順利
時，幾乎任何人都可以和你一起笑一笑；但當你真的遇到困難時，往往只有那一小圈真正親
近的人才會站出來。對我來說，友情不太是關於數量，而更是關於深度。幾個親密朋友，有
時就像在不太穩定的世界裡，給你一塊踏實的地。]

components.english: For example, if I were going through a stressful period, I would rather have two or three close friends who know me well, listen properly, and tell me the truth, than ten people who only know the polished version of me. Close friends can read between the lines, and that kind of understanding is worth its weight in gold.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我正經歷一段壓力很大的時期，我會更希望自己身邊有兩三個真正了解我、願意認真聆聽我，並且願意坦白對我說真話的朋友，而不是十個只認識表面那個我的人。親密朋友往往能聽懂那些沒有直接說出口的意思，而那種理解其實非常珍貴。

rather have two or three close friends who know me well, listen properly, and
tell me the truth, than ten people who only know the polished version of me.
Close friends can read between the lines, and that kind of understanding is
worth its weight in gold.

components.english: So, if I had to choose, I would definitely go for a few very good friends, because real friendship is about connection, not collection.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要選，我一定會選幾個很好的朋友，因為真正的友情重點在於連結，而不是收集人數。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose, I would definitely go for a few very good
friends, because real friendship is about connection, not collection.
[所以，如果一定要選，我一定會選幾個很好的朋友，因為真正的友情重點在於連結，而不
是收集人數。]

components.english: That said, I can still see the value of having a wider circle of friends, because different people can bring different energy, perspectives, and opportunities into your life.
components.chinese: 不過，我也看得到擁有較大朋友圈的價值，因為不同的人確實能為人生帶來不同的能量、觀點，以及機會。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I can still see the value of having a wider circle of friends,
because different people can bring different energy, perspectives, and
opportunities into your life.
[不過，我也看得到擁有較大朋友圈的價值，因為不同的人確實能為人生帶來不同的能量、
觀點，以及機會。]

components.english: Casual friendships can make life more lively and open more doors socially. They can introduce you to new hobbies, new ideas, and even new stages of life. So I would not say having many friends is a bad thing at all. It is just that a wide circle may colour life, whereas close friends help hold it together. In other words, it is nice to know many people, but it is even better to be truly known by a few.
components.chinese: 普通朋友可以令生活更熱鬧，也能在社交上打開更多門。他們可能會把你帶進新的興趣、新的想法，甚至新的人生階段。所以我絕不會說有很多朋友是一件壞事。只不過，廣闊的朋友圈也許能替生活增添色彩，而親密朋友則更像是在幫你把生活穩穩撐住。換句話說，認識很多人固然很好，但被少數幾個人真正了解，其實更難得。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Casual friendships can make life more lively and open more
doors socially. They can introduce you to new hobbies, new ideas, and even new

友圈也許能替生活增添色彩，而親密朋友則更像是在幫你把生活穩穩撐住。換句話說，認識
很多人固然很好，但被少數幾個人真正了解，其實更難得。]

components.english: For instance, at school or at work, it is helpful to get along with many people and build a friendly social network. That can make daily life easier and more enjoyable. Still, when it comes to sharing your fears, your failures, or the parts of yourself you do not show to everyone, you usually turn to the people who are closest to your heart.
components.chinese: 例如，在學校或工作場合裡，和很多人相處融洽、建立友善的人際網絡，的確是有幫助的。這會令日常生活更順利，也更愉快。不過，當事情來到要分享自己的恐懼、失敗，或者那些不會對每個人展示的一面時，人通常還是會轉向那些最貼近自己內心的人。

components.english: Overall, I’d say I would rather have a few very good friends than a lot of just friends. A crowd can make life busier, but a handful of real friends can make it richer.

question.english: If you had a problem, would you go to your friends or family? Why?
question.chinese: 主題：如果你遇到問題，你會去找朋友還是家人？為甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: If you had a problem, would you go to your friends or family? Why?
主題：如果你遇到問題，你會去找朋友還是家人？為甚麼？

components.english: Personally, I would probably go to my family first, especially if the problem were serious, because family tends to be the people who know me most deeply and care about the outcome most sincerely.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我大概會先找家人，尤其如果那個問題比較嚴重，因為家人通常是最了解我的人，也是真心最在乎結果的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I would probably go to my family first, especially if the
problem were serious, because family tends to be the people who know me most
deeply and care about the outcome most sincerely.
[就我個人而言，我大概會先找家人，尤其如果那個問題比較嚴重，因為家人通常是最了解
我的人，也是真心最在乎結果的人。]

components.english: The main reason is that when something really goes wrong, I do not just need advice; I need steadiness. Family can often provide that emotional anchor. They may not always say exactly what I want to hear, but they are more likely to tell me what I need to hear. In that sense, going to family can feel less like asking for an opinion and more like finding solid ground when life starts to wobble.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，當事情真的出問題時，我需要的往往不只是建議，而是一種穩定感。家人很多時都能提供那種情緒上的支撐。他們未必總是說出我最想聽的話，但更有可能說出我真正需要聽的話。從這個角度來看，找家人傾訴，不只是向別人問意見，更像是在生活開始搖晃時重新找到站穩的地方。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that when something really goes wrong, I do
not just need advice; I need steadiness. Family can often provide that emotional
anchor. They may not always say exactly what I want to hear, but they are more
likely to tell me what I need to hear. In that sense, going to family can feel less
like asking for an opinion and more like finding solid ground when life starts to
wobble.
[主要原因是，當事情真的出問題時，我需要的往往不只是建議，而是一種穩定感。家人很
多時都能提供那種情緒上的支撐。他們未必總是說出我最想聽的話，但更有可能說出我真
正需要聽的話。從這個角度來看，找家人傾訴，不只是向別人問意見，更像是在生活開始搖
晃時重新找到站穩的地方。]

components.english: For example, if I had a major decision to make, such as changing jobs, dealing with a financial problem, or facing a health issue, I would be much more likely to talk to my parents or another close family member. They tend to see the bigger picture and think about my long-term well-being rather than just the mood of the moment.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我要面對一個重大的決定，例如轉工、處理財務問題，或者應付健康上的困擾，我通常會更傾向和父母，或者其他親近家人商量。他們往往會看得更長遠，也會更重視我整體和長期的福祉，而不只是當下的情緒。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if I had a major decision to make, such as changing
jobs, dealing with a financial problem, or facing a health issue, I would be much
more likely to talk to my parents or another close family member. They tend to
see the bigger picture and think about my long-term well-being rather than just
the mood of the moment.
[例如，如果我要面對一個重大的決定，例如轉工、處理財務問題，或者應付健康上的困擾，
我通常會更傾向和父母，或者其他親近家人商量。他們往往會看得更長遠，也會更重視我整
體和長期的福祉，而不只是當下的情緒。]

components.english: So, if the problem were important, I would usually turn to my family first, because they can offer both honesty and a sense of security.
components.chinese: 所以，如果那個問題比較重要，我通常會先找家人，因為他們往往既能給我坦白的意見，也能給我一種安全感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if the problem were important, I would usually turn to my
family first, because they can offer both honesty and a sense of security.
[所以，如果那個問題比較重要，我通常會先找家人，因為他們往往既能給我坦白的意見，
也能給我一種安全感。]

components.english: That said, I would still go to friends for certain kinds of problems, especially when I needed emotional release, practical advice from someone my own age, or simply a person who could relate more directly.
components.chinese: 不過，對某些類型的問題，我仍然會找朋友，尤其當我需要先抒發情緒、想聽同齡人的實際建議，或者只是想找一個更能直接明白我處境的人時。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would still go to friends for certain kinds of problems,
especially when I needed emotional release, practical advice from someone my
own age, or simply a person who could relate more directly.
[不過，對某些類型的問題，我仍然會找朋友，尤其當我需要先抒發情緒、想聽同齡人的實
際建議，或者只是想找一個更能直接明白我處境的人時。]

components.english: To be honest, friends and family play different roles. Friends are often easier to open up to about things like relationship stress, study pressure, or awkward personal situations, because they are closer to the rhythm of your daily life. Sometimes they speak the same emotional language, so to speak, and that can make you feel instantly understood. A family member may give you depth, but a friend may give you immediacy.
components.chinese: 老實說，朋友和家人扮演的角色其實不太一樣。對於感情壓力、學業煩惱，或者一些比較尷尬的個人處境，朋友有時反而更容易讓人開口，因為他們更貼近你日常生活的節奏。有時候，他們就像是在說同一種情緒語言，所以你會很快覺得自己被理解。家人也許能給你深度，而朋友很多時則能給你即時的共鳴。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, friends and family play different roles. Friends
are often easier to open up to about things like relationship stress, study
pressure, or awkward personal situations, because they are closer to the rhythm
of your daily life. Sometimes they speak the same emotional language, so to
speak, and that can make you feel instantly understood. A family member may
give you depth, but a friend may give you immediacy.
[老實說，朋友和家人扮演的角色其實不太一樣。對於感情壓力、學業煩惱，或者一些比較
尷尬的個人處境，朋友有時反而更容易讓人開口，因為他們更貼近你日常生活的節奏。有時
候，他們就像是在說同一種情緒語言，所以你會很快覺得自己被理解。家人也許能給你深度
，而朋友很多時則能給你即時的共鳴。]

components.english: For instance, if I were feeling overwhelmed by university work or upset after an argument with someone, I might message a close friend first and talk it through over coffee. That kind of conversation can help take the weight off your shoulders before you decide what to do next.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我因為大學功課而忙得喘不過氣，或者和別人吵架之後心情很差，我可能會先傳訊息給一位好朋友，約他出來喝杯咖啡，好好聊一聊。這樣的對話有時能先幫你卸下一點壓力，然後你再決定下一步怎樣做。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if I were feeling overwhelmed by university work or
upset after an argument with someone, I might message a close friend first and
talk it through over coffee. That kind of conversation can help take the weight
off your shoulders before you decide what to do next.
[例如，如果我因為大學功課而忙得喘不過氣，或者和別人吵架之後心情很差，我可能會先
傳訊息給一位好朋友，約他出來喝杯咖啡，好好聊一聊。這樣的對話有時能先幫你卸下一點
壓力，然後你再決定下一步怎樣做。]

components.english: Overall, I would say I would go to family for bigger, deeper problems, but to friends for more immediate and everyday ones. Both matter, but I tend to go to family when I need a foundation, and to friends when I need understanding in the moment.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，面對較大、較深層的問題時，我會找家人；至於一些比較即時、比較日常的煩惱，我則更可能找朋友。兩者都很重要，只不過當我需要的是根基和穩定時，我會找家人；當我需要的是當下的理解和共鳴時，我會找朋友。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say I would go to family for bigger, deeper
problems, but to friends for more immediate and everyday ones. Both matter,
but I tend to go to family when I need a foundation, and to friends when I need
understanding in the moment.
[總結來說，我會說，面對較大、較深層的問題時，我會找家人；至於一些比較即時、比較日
常的煩惱，我則更可能找朋友。兩者都很重要，只不過當我需要的是根基和穩定時，我會找
家人；當我需要的是當下的理解和共鳴時，我會找朋友。]

question.english: Do you think it is always better to talk to your friends about such a problem?
question.chinese: 主題：你認為遇到這類問題時，總是和朋友傾談會比較好嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think it is always better to talk to your friends about such a problem?
主題：你認為遇到這類問題時，總是和朋友傾談會比較好嗎？

components.english: No, not always. I think talking to friends is often helpful, but whether it is the best option really depends on the nature of the problem.
components.chinese: 不一定。我認為和朋友傾談很多時候是有幫助的，但這是否是最好的做法，其實很取決於那個問題本身的性質。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: No, not always. I think talking to friends is often helpful, but whether
it is the best option really depends on the nature of the problem.
[不一定。我認為和朋友傾談很多時候是有幫助的，但這是否是最好的做法，其實很取決於
那個問題本身的性質。]

components.english: Friends can be a safe place to land when life feels heavy. They know your personality, your history, and the little things that outsiders might miss, so they can often offer comfort, reassurance, and a different perspective. Sometimes, simply saying a problem out loud can take a weight off your shoulders. In that sense, a good friend can act like an emotional anchor when everything else feels a bit shaky.
components.chinese: 當生活讓人感到沉重時，朋友很多時候就像一個讓人可以安心停靠的地方。他們了解你的性格、你的過去，也知道一些外人未必看得見的小細節，所以往往能提供安慰、鼓勵，以及另一種看事情的角度。有時候，單單把問題說出口，就已經能讓肩上的重擔輕一點。從這個角度來看，當一切都顯得有點搖晃時，一位好朋友確實可以像情感上的錨一樣，把人穩住。

components.english: For example, if someone is upset after an argument, stressed about exams, or confused about a relationship, talking to a close friend can help them clear their head. A friend may not solve the whole issue, but they can help the person feel less alone and less trapped in their own thoughts.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人因為和別人爭執而心情低落、為考試感到壓力，或者對一段關係感到困惑，那麼和親密朋友傾談，往往能幫助他整理思緒。朋友也許不能把整個問題完全解決，但他們能讓那個人感到自己不是孤軍作戰，也不會一直被困在自己的想法裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone is upset after an argument, stressed about

components.english: So, for many everyday emotional problems, talking to friends can be a very good first step.
components.chinese: 所以，對很多日常情緒上的問題來說，和朋友傾談的確可以是一個很好的第一步。

be a very good first step.

components.english: That said, I do not think friends are always enough, because some problems need more than kindness and good intentions.

components.english: If the issue is serious, such as depression, abuse, addiction, legal trouble, or major financial stress, a friend may mean well but still be out of their depth. A good heart is valuable, but it is not the same as professional judgement. On top of that, friends are sometimes too emotionally involved to be objective, so their advice can be warm but blurry. In other words, friends can hold your hand, but they cannot always lead the way.
components.chinese: 如果問題本身很嚴重，例如抑鬱、受虐、成癮、法律麻煩，或者重大的經濟壓力，那麼朋友即使出於好意，也可能仍然應付不來。一顆善良的心固然珍貴，但它並不等於專業判斷。除此之外，朋友有時因為和你情感太接近，反而未必能保持客觀，所以他們的建議也許很溫暖，卻未必很清晰。換句話說，朋友可以牽著你的手，但未必總能帶你走出迷霧。

，卻未必很清晰。換句話說，朋友可以牽著你的手，但未必總能帶你走出迷霧。]

components.english: For instance, if someone is struggling with severe anxiety or is trapped in a toxic relationship, talking to a friend may provide comfort, but that person might still need a counsellor, teacher, social worker, or some other qualified adult. In that kind of case, relying on friendship alone may be like bringing a torch when what you really need is a map.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人正受嚴重焦慮困擾，或者被困在一段有毒的關係裡，那麼和朋友傾談雖然能帶來安慰，但那個人可能仍然需要輔導員、老師、社工，或者其他有專業能力的成年人幫助。在這種情況下，只靠友情本身，很多時就像手上有一支電筒，但你真正需要的其實是一張地圖。

components.english: Overall, I would say it is often helpful to talk to friends, but it is not always the best solution. Friends can be a wonderful first line of support, but when the problem runs deep, wisdom sometimes means knowing when to look beyond friendship as well.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說和朋友傾談通常是有幫助的，但它並不總是最好的解決方法。朋友可以成為非常重要的第一道支持，但當問題本身很深、很重的時候，真正的成熟有時也在於知道何時要把目光放到友情以外的幫助上。

question.english: Is it important to have friends from other countries?
question.chinese: 擁有來自其他國家的朋友是否重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it important to have friends from other countries?
擁有來自其他國家的朋友是否重要？

components.english: Yes, I do think it is important, because friends from other countries can broaden your mind and help you see life from a different angle.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為這是重要的，因為來自其他國家的朋友能開闊一個人的眼界，也能讓你從不同角度去看生活。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think it is important, because friends from other countries can
broaden your mind and help you see life from a different angle.
[是的，我的確認為這是重要的，因為來自其他國家的朋友能開闊一個人的眼界，也能讓你
從不同角度去看生活。]

components.english: The main reason is that friendship can open a window onto another culture in a way that books or videos often cannot. When you are close to someone from a different background, you begin to understand how they think, what they value, and how daily life looks from their side of the world. That kind of contact can chip away at stereotypes and make people more open-minded. In other words, the world starts to feel a little less foreign and a little more human.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，友情能以一種書本或影片未必做得到的方式，為你打開通往另一種文化的窗。當你和一位來自不同背景的人變得親近時，你會開始明白他們怎樣思考、重視甚麼，以及他們眼中的日常生活是甚麼樣子。這種接觸能慢慢打破刻板印象，令一個人變得更開放。換句話說，這個世界會開始少一點陌生感，多一點人情味。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that friendship can open a window onto
another culture in a way that books or videos often cannot. When you are close
to someone from a different background, you begin to understand how they
think, what they value, and how daily life looks from their side of the world.
That kind of contact can chip away at stereotypes and make people more
open-minded. In other words, the world starts to feel a little less foreign and a
little more human.
[主要原因是，友情能以一種書本或影片未必做得到的方式，為你打開通往另一種文化的
窗。當你和一位來自不同背景的人變得親近時，你會開始明白他們怎樣思考、重視甚麼，以
及他們眼中的日常生活是甚麼樣子。這種接觸能慢慢打破刻板印象，令一個人變得更開放。
換句話說，這個世界會開始少一點陌生感，多一點人情味。]

components.english: For example, if someone has a close friend from Japan, Brazil, or France, they may naturally learn about different food habits, communication styles, festivals, or even attitudes towards work and family. That kind of learning does not feel forced at all; it just grows out of ordinary conversation and shared experience.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人有一位來自日本、巴西，或者法國的好朋友，他很自然就可能會接觸到不同的飲食習慣、溝通方式、節日文化，甚至對工作和家庭的看法。這種學習完全不會讓人覺得刻意，因為它只是從平常對話和共同經歷裡慢慢長出來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone has a close friend from Japan, Brazil, or
France, they may naturally learn about different food habits, communication
styles, festivals, or even attitudes towards work and family. That kind of
learning does not feel forced at all; it just grows out of ordinary conversation
and shared experience.
[例如，如果一個人有一位來自日本、巴西，或者法國的好朋友，他很自然就可能會接觸到
不同的飲食習慣、溝通方式、節日文化，甚至對工作和家庭的看法。這種學習完全不會讓人
覺得刻意，因為它只是從平常對話和共同經歷裡慢慢長出來。]

components.english: So, in that sense, having friends from other countries is important, because it helps people grow in understanding, empathy, and perspective.
components.chinese: 所以，從這個角度來看，擁有來自其他國家的朋友是重要的，因為這能幫助人們在理解力、同理心，以及看事情的角度上成長。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in that sense, having friends from other countries is
important, because it helps people grow in understanding, empathy, and
perspective.
[所以，從這個角度來看，擁有來自其他國家的朋友是重要的，因為這能幫助人們在理解
力、同理心，以及看事情的角度上成長。]

components.english: On top of that, I think international friendships can also be personally rewarding, because they make life richer and sometimes more exciting.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為，跨國友情在個人層面上也很有價值，因為它會令生活變得更豐富，有時也更有趣。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think international friendships can also be
personally rewarding, because they make life richer and sometimes more
exciting.
[除此之外，我也認為，跨國友情在個人層面上也很有價值，因為它會令生活變得更豐富，
有時也更有趣。]

components.english: Friends from other countries can introduce you to new ideas, new humour, new ways of speaking, and new ways of looking at the world. They can also bring people out of their comfort zone in a good way. Sometimes a friendship like that becomes a bridge rather than just a bond: it connects not only two people, but also two different worlds. That said, I would not say it is essential in some absolute sense. What matters most is not the passport, but the quality of the friendship. Still, if people do have the chance to build those connections, I think it is something genuinely valuable.
components.chinese: 來自其他國家的朋友可以把新的想法、新的幽默感、新的說話方式，以及新的世界觀帶進你的生活裡。他們也能以一種正面的方式，把人帶離自己的舒適圈。有時，這樣的友情不只是一段關係，更像一座橋樑：它連接的不只是兩個人，而是兩個不同的世界。不過，我也不會說這在某種絕對意義上是不可缺少的。最重要的始終不是護照來自哪裡，而是友情本身的質素。只是如果一個人有機會建立這樣的連結，我認為那確實是一件很有價值的事。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Friends from other countries can introduce you to new ideas,
new humour, new ways of speaking, and new ways of looking at the world. They
can also bring people out of their comfort zone in a good way. Sometimes a
friendship like that becomes a bridge rather than just a bond: it connects not
only two people, but also two different worlds. That said, I would not say it is
essential in some absolute sense. What matters most is not the passport, but the
quality of the friendship. Still, if people do have the chance to build those
connections, I think it is something genuinely valuable.
[來自其他國家的朋友可以把新的想法、新的幽默感、新的說話方式，以及新的世界觀帶進
你的生活裡。他們也能以一種正面的方式，把人帶離自己的舒適圈。有時，這樣的友情不只
是一段關係，更像一座橋樑：它連接的不只是兩個人，而是兩個不同的世界。不過，我也不
會說這在某種絕對意義上是不可缺少的。最重要的始終不是護照來自哪裡，而是友情本身
的質素。只是如果一個人有機會建立這樣的連結，我認為那確實是一件很有價值的事。]

components.english: For instance, if you travel abroad and already know someone there, the place may no longer feel like just a destination on a map. It feels warmer, more alive, and more personal. Even staying at home, you might celebrate a foreign festival with them, pick up a few phrases in their language, or simply laugh at how differently both of you see the same situation.
components.chinese: 例如，如果你去外國旅行，而你剛好已經認識那裡的一位朋友，那個地方對你來說就不再只是地圖上的一個目的地，而會變得更溫暖、更有生命感，也更有個人意義。即使你留在自己居住的地方，你也可能會和對方一起過外國節日、學幾句他們的語言，或者單純因為大家對同一件事有不同看法而笑出來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if you travel abroad and already know someone
there, the place may no longer feel like just a destination on a map. It feels
warmer, more alive, and more personal. Even staying at home, you might
celebrate a foreign festival with them, pick up a few phrases in their language,
or simply laugh at how differently both of you see the same situation.
